questions.subject: Polity
statementLines: With reference to Home Guards, consider the following statements:
1. Home Guards are raised under the Home Guards Act and Rules of the Central Government.
2. The role of the Home Guards is to serve as an auxiliary force to the police in maintenance of internal security.
3. To prevent infiltration on the international border/ coastal areas, the Border Wing Home Guards Battalions have been raised in some States.
How many of the above statements are correct?
questions.questionText: With reference to Home Guards, consider the following statements: 1. Home Guards are raised under the Home Guards Act and Rules of the Central Government. 2. The role of the Home Guards is to serve as an auxiliary force to the police in maintenance of internal security. 3. To prevent infiltration on the international border/ coastal areas, the Border Wing Home Guards Battalions have been raised in some States. How many of the above statements are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. **Home Guards** is a voluntary force, first raised in India in December **1946**, to assist the police in controlling civil disturbance and communal riots.
**Statement 1 is incorrect: Home Guards are raised under the**Home Guards Act and Rules of the States/Union Territories**(not central Government).** They are recruited from amongst all classes of people and walks of life, who give their spare time to the organisation for betterment of the community.
**Statement 2 is correct: The role of Home Guards is to serve as an auxiliary Force to the Police in maintenance of internal security situations, help the community in any kind of emergency such as an air-raid, fire, cyclone, earthquake, epidemic etc., help in maintenance of essential services, promote communal harmony and assist the administration in protecting weaker sections, participate in socio-economic and welfare activities and perform Civil Defence duties.**

**Statement 3 is correct: Fifteen**Border Wing Home Guards (BWHG)**Battalions have been raised in the border States viz.** Punjab (6 Bns.), Rajasthan (4 Bns.), Gujarat (2 Bns.) and one each Battalion for Meghalaya, Tripura and West Bengal to serve as an auxiliary to Border Security Force for preventing infiltration on the international border/coastal areas, guarding of VA/VPs and lines of communication in vulnerable area at the time of external aggression.
Therefore, only two statements are correct.

questions.subject: Polity
statementLines: With reference to India, consider the following pairs: Action - The Act under which it is covered
1. Unauthorized wearing of police or military uniforms - The Official Secrets Act, 1923
2. Knowingly misleading or otherwise interfering with a police officer or military officer when engaged in their duties - The Indian Evidence Act, 1872
3. Celebratory gunfire which can endanger the personal safety of others - The Arms (Amendment) Act, 2019
How many of the above pairs are correctly matched?
questions.questionText: With reference to India, consider the following pairs: Action - The Act under which it is covered 1. Unauthorized wearing of police or military uniforms - The Official Secrets Act, 1923 2. Knowingly misleading or otherwise interfering with a police officer or military officer when engaged in their duties - The Indian Evidence Act, 1872 3. Celebratory gunfire which can endanger the personal safety of others - The Arms (Amendment) Act, 2019 How many of the above pairs are correctly matched?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer.

**Pair 1 is correctly matched:**The Official Secrets Act, 1923**prohibits person from using or wearing, without lawful authority, any naval, military, air force, police or other official uniform, or any uniform so nearly resembling the same as to be calculated to deceive, or falsely represents himself to be a person who is or has been entitled to use or wear any such uniform**

**Pair 2 is incorrectly matched: It is under**The Official Secrets Act, 1923**, that no person in the vicinity of any prohibited place shall obstruct, knowingly mislead or otherwise interfere with or impede, any police officer, or any member of 21 [the Armed Forces of the Union] engaged on guard, sentry, patrol or other similar duty in relation to the prohibited place.**

**Pair 3 is correctly matched:**The Arms Act Amendment of 2019**adds news offences such as forcefully taking a firearm from police or armed forces and using firearms in a celebratory gunfire which endangers human life or personal safety of others.**

questions.subject: Polity
statementLines: Consider the following statements in respect of the National Flag of India according to the Flag Code of India, 2002:
Statement-I: One of the standard sizes of the National Flag of India is 600 mm x 400 mm.
Statement-II: The ratio of the length to the height (width) of the Flag shall be 3:2.
Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements in respect of the National Flag of India according to the Flag Code of India, 2002: Statement-I: One of the standard sizes of the National Flag of India is 600 mm x 400 mm. Statement-II: The ratio of the length to the height (width) of the Flag shall be 3:2. Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?

options.a: Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct and Statement-II is the correct explanation for Statement-I
options.b: Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct and Statement-II is not the correct explanation for Statement-I
options.c: Statement-I is correct but Statement-II is incorrect
options.d: Statement-I is incorrect but Statement-II is correct

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. The use, display and hoisting of the National Flag in the country is guided by an overarching set of instructions called the **'Flag Code of India**2002**'**.
The code brings together all laws, conventions, practices, and instructions for the display of the National Flag.

**Statement 1 is incorrect: As per the Flag code of India**2002**, the National Flag cannot be in the dimension of 600mm*400mm.** The code states that the National flag can be of nine standard dimensions as follows:
1. 1-6300 x 4200 2. 2-3600 x 2400 3. 3-2700 x 1800 4. 4-1800 x 1200 5. 5-1350 x 900 6. 6-900 x 600 7. 7-450 x 300 8. 8-225 x 150 9. 9-150 x 100

**Statement 2 is correct: The National Flag shall be rectangular in shape and the ratio of the length to the height (width) of the Flag shall be 3:2.** Hence

Statement 2 is correct.

questions.subject: Polity
statementLines: With reference to anti-defection law in India, consider the following statements:
1. The law specifies that a nominated legislator cannot join any political party within six months of being appointed to the House.
2. The law does not provide any time-frame within which the presiding officer has to decide a defection case.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: With reference to anti-defection law in India, consider the following statements: 1. The law specifies that a nominated legislator cannot join any political party within six months of being appointed to the House. 2. The law does not provide any time-frame within which the presiding officer has to decide a defection case. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer.

**Statement 1 is incorrect: A**nominated member**of a House shall be disqualified for being a member of the House if he joins any political party after the expiry of six months from the date on which he takes his seat.** Thus he/she is free to join any political party within six months and would not be disqualified for the same.
**Statement 2 is correct: The law does not specify a time period for the Presiding Officer to decide on a disqualification plea under the**Tenth Schedule**.**

questions.subject: Polity
statementLines: With reference to the "Tea Board" in India, consider the following statements:
1. The Tea Board is a statutory body.
2. It is a regulatory body attached to the Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare.
3. The Tea Board's Head Office is situated in Bengaluru.
4. The Board has overseas office at Dubai and Moscow.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.questionText: With reference to the "Tea Board" in India, consider the following statements: 1. The Tea Board is a statutory body. 2. It is a regulatory body attached to the Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare. 3. The Tea Board's Head Office is situated in Bengaluru. 4. The Board has overseas office at Dubai and Moscow. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer.

**Statement 1 is correct: The**Tea Board of India**is a statutory body created under the**Tea Act, 1953**and it was established for the purposes of regulating the Indian tea industry and protecting the interests of tea producers in India.**

**Statement 2 is incorrect: It is functioning as a statutory body of the Central Government under the**Ministry of Commerce**.**

**Statement 3 is incorrect: Tea Board of Indias Head Office is situated in**Kolkata**.**

**Statement 4 is correct: The Tea Board of India has overseas offices in**Moscow, Dubai, Hamburg, London and New York**.** Moscow office. The Moscow office of the Tea Board of India operates under the Embassy of India, and its area of activity includes Russia and the CIS countries, which comprise 50% of Indian tea exports.

questions.subject: Polity
statementLines: Consider the following statements:
1. "Right to the city" is an agreed human right and the UN-Habitat monitors the Commitments made by each country in this regard.
2. "Right to the City" gives every occupant of the city the right to reclaim public spaces and public participation in the city.
3. "Right to the City" means that the State cannot deny any public service or facility to the unauthorized colonies in the city.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements: 1. "Right to the city" is an agreed human right and the UN-Habitat monitors the Commitments made by each country in this regard. 2. "Right to the City" gives every occupant of the city the right to reclaim public spaces and public participation in the city. 3. "Right to the City" means that the State cannot deny any public service or facility to the unauthorized colonies in the city. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. The **right to the city** is individual and collective right of all residents of a city/town/village to an equal share of its benefits and to participate in its development.
It is not to be viewed as a new legalistic right, but merely as an articulation to consolidate the demand for the realisation of multiple human rights.
It developed as a movement for demand of basic rights to city dwellers by Global NGOs.

**Statement 1 is Incorrect: Right to the city has developed as a response of social groups and civil society organizations to ensure better access to and opportunities for everyone living in cities, especially the most marginalized and deprived sections.** Social movements and organizations from across the world worked together to develop a **World Charter on the Right to the City** that is also supported by UNESCO and UN Habitat, among other agencies.
Hence UN HABITAT only supports and doesnot monitor the commitments made by each country in this regard. Statements 2 and 3 are correct: The Right to city demand is based on basic human rights principles of-nondiscrimination;
indivisibility of human rights; gender equality; progressive realisation; non-retrogression, subsidiarity; solidarity; and, cooperation. It emphasises priority attention to persons and groups in situations of vulnerability, including the homeless/landless, children, persons with disabilities, religious and sexual minorities, older persons, informal sector workers, Dalits/members of Scheduled Castes, indigenous and tribal peoples, persons from economically weaker sections, single women, and women-headed households, among others.
It also stresses responsibility and sustainability as core principles. The concept of the right to the city is a means to combat the exclusionary development, selective benefit sharing, marginalisation, and discrimination rampant in cities today.

questions.subject: Polity
statementLines: Consider the following statements in respect of Bharat Ratna and Padma Awards:
1. Bharat Ratna and Padma awards are titles under the Article 18 (1) of the Constitution of India.
2. Padma Awards, which were instituted in the year 1954, were suspended only once.
3. The number of Bharat Ratna Awards is restricted to a maximum of five in a particular year.
Which of the above statements are not correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements in respect of Bharat Ratna and Padma Awards: 1. Bharat Ratna and Padma awards are titles under the Article 18 (1) of the Constitution of India. 2. Padma Awards, which were instituted in the year 1954, were suspended only once. 3. The number of Bharat Ratna Awards is restricted to a maximum of five in a particular year. Which of the above statements are not correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer.

**Statement 1 is incorrect. National awards such as**Bharat Ratna**, Padma Vibhushan, Padma Bhushan and Padma Shri do not amount to titles within the meaning of**Article 18(1)**of the Constitution and thus are not to be used as prefixes or suffixes to the name of the recipient in any manner. Article 18(1)** abolishes all titles. It prohibits the State to confer titles on anybody whether a citizen or a non-citizen.
Military and academic distinctions are, however, exempted from the prohibition. Thus, a university can give title or honor on a man of merit.
**Statement 2 is incorrect.**Padma Awards**were instituted in**1954**to be awarded to citizens of India in recognition of their distinguished contribution in various spheres of activity.** The government suspended the practice of granting the Padma awards for two years in **1977**. It was again suspended during mid
- **1992** when 2 PILs were filed in the High Courts of India
They are given in three categories: Padma Vibhushan (for exceptional and distinguished service), Padma Bhushan (distinguished service of higher-order) and Padma Shri (distinguished service).
**Statement 3 is incorrect. A maximum of 3 people can be awarded the**Bharat Ratna**.** The total number of Padma awards to be conferred each year is limited to 120. But the count excludes posthumous awards and any non-resident Indian or Overseas Citizen of India or foreign-based winners.

questions.subject: Polity
statementLines: Consider the following statements:
1. Aadhaar metadata cannot be stored for more than three months.
2. State cannot enter into any contract with private corporations for sharing of Aadhaar data.
3. Aadhaar is mandatory for obtaining insurance products.
4. Aadhaar is mandatory for getting benefits funded out of the Consolidated Fund of India.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements: 1. Aadhaar metadata cannot be stored for more than three months. 2. State cannot enter into any contract with private corporations for sharing of Aadhaar data. 3. Aadhaar is mandatory for obtaining insurance products. 4. Aadhaar is mandatory for getting benefits funded out of the Consolidated Fund of India. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer.

**Statement 1 is incorrect. A Constitution Bench of the Supreme Court ruled that the**Aadhaar metadata**cannot be stored beyond six months.** The Aadhar Act had allowed storage of such data for five years.
**Statement 2 is correct. Supreme Court has struck down**Section 57**that allowed sharing of data with private entities.** But has allowed the unique number to be used for government schemes and subsidies.
**Statement 3 is incorrect. Aadhaar is not mandatory for obtaining insurance products.** Insurance regulator **IRDAI** has advised insurers not to mandatorily seek Aadhaar and PAN/Form 60 from customers, existing or new, for KYC (Know Your Customer) purpose.
The insurers can accept Aadhaar as one of the documents for KYC, only when the same is offered voluntarily by the proposer/policy-holder.

**Statement 4 is correct. The amendment made to Section 7 of the Aadhaar (Targeted delivery of financial and other subsidies, benefits and services) Act**2016**, make Aadhar mandatory for beneficiaries for receipt of a subsidy, benefit or service for which the expenditure is incurred from the**Consolidated Fund**.**

questions.subject: Polity
statementLines: Along with the Budget, the Finance Minister also places other documents before the Parliament which include "The Macro Economic Framework Statement'. The aforesaid document is presented because this is mandated by
questions.questionText: Along with the Budget, the Finance Minister also places other documents before the Parliament which include "The Macro Economic Framework Statement'. The aforesaid document is presented because this is mandated by

options.b: Article 112 and Article 110(1) of the Constitution of India
options.c: Article 113 of the Constitution of India
options.d: Provisions of the Fiscal Responsibility and Budget Management Act, 2003

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. The **Fiscal Responsibility and Budget Management Act, 2003** mandates that Central Government shall lay in each financial year before both Houses of Parliament the following statements (along with the annual financial statement):
(a) the Medium-term Fiscal Policy Statement; (b) the Fiscal Policy Strategy Statement; (c) the **Macro-economic Framework Statement**; (d) the Medium-term Expenditure Framework Statement. **Macro-economic Framework Statement** provides an overview of the economy containing an assessment regarding the fiscal balance of the Central Government, Price related fluctuations, GDP growth rate, and the external sector balance of the economy.

questions.subject: Polity
statementLines: One common agreement between Gandhism and Marxism is:
questions.questionText: One common agreement between Gandhism and Marxism is:

options.a: The final goal of a stateless society

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. One common agreement between **Gandhism** and **Marxism** is the final goal of **stateless and classless society**, whereas means to achieve these final goals differ.
For Marx, State is an instrument of oppression and an organ of the bourgeoisie that only works for maintaining the class dominance. Therefore, he strives for class struggle and revolution which will ultimately result in a stateless society. For Gandhi also, in an ideal state, there is no State.
In his words political power means capacity to regulate national life through national representatives. If national life becomes so perfect as to become self-regulated, no representation becomes necessary. There is then a state of enlightened anarchy. In such a state everyone is his own ruler. He rules himself in such a manner that is never a hindrance to his neighbour. In the ideal state, therefore, there is no political power because there is no State.

questions.subject: Polity
statementLines: Consider the following statements:
1. The Parliament (Prevention of Disqualification) Act, 1959 exempts several posts from disqualification on the grounds of 'Office of Profit'.
2. The above-mentioned Act was amended five times.
3. The term 'Office of Profit' is well-defined in the Constitution of India.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements: 1. The Parliament (Prevention of Disqualification) Act, 1959 exempts several posts from disqualification on the grounds of 'Office of Profit'. 2. The above-mentioned Act was amended five times. 3. The term 'Office of Profit' is well-defined in the Constitution of India. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer.

**Statement 1 is correct. Section 3 of the**Parliament (Prevention of Disqualification) Act, 1959**lists certain offices of profit under the central and state governments, which do not disqualify the holders from being an MP.** It exempts posts like:
(i) Ministers of State and Deputy Ministers (ii) Parliamentary Secretaries and Parliamentary Under Secretaries, (iii) Deputy Chief Whips in Parliament, (iv) Vice-Chancellors of Universities, (v) Officers in the National Cadet Corps, and the Territorial Army, and (vi) the Chairman and members of Advisory Committees set up by the Government when they are not entitled to any fee or remuneration other than compensatory Etc.
**Statement 2 is correct. The**Parliament (Prevention of Disqualification) Act, 1959**has been amended 5 times since its formulation (in**1960**,**1992**,**1993**,**2006**and**2013**).**

**Statement 3 is incorrect.**Article 102**of the Constitution of India provides that a person shall be disqualified from his membership of parliament if he holds any office of profit under the Government of India or the Government of any State.** However, the **'Office of Profit'** is not defined in the Constitution of India.

questions.subject: Polity
statementLines: For election to the Lok Sabha, a nomination paper can be filed by:
questions.questionText: For election to the Lok Sabha, a nomination paper can be filed by:

options.b: A resident of the constituency from which the election is to be contested.
options.c: Any citizen of India whose name appears in the electoral roll of a constituency.

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. According to section 4 (d) of the **Representation of People Act, 1951**, for election to Lok Sabha, a nomination paper can be filed by any citizen whose name appears in the electoral rolls of any parliamentary constituency.
The Constitution lays down the following **qualifications** for a person to be chosen a member of the Parliament: 1) He must be a citizen of India.
2) He must make and subscribe to an oath or affirmation before the person authorised by the election commission for this purpose. In his oath or affirmation, he swears (a) To bear true faith and allegiance to the Constitution of India (b) To uphold the sovereignty and integrity of India 3) He must be not less than 30 years of age in the case of the Rajya Sabha and not less than 25 years of age in the case of the Lok Sabha.
4) He must possess other qualifications prescribed by Parliament. The Parliament has laid down the following additional qualifications in the Representation of People Act (**1951**).
1) He must be registered as an elector for a parliamentary constituency. This is same in the case of both, the Rajya Sabha and the Lok Sabha.
2) He must be a member of a scheduled caste or scheduled tribe in any state or union territory, if he wants to contest a seat reserved for them.
However, a member of scheduled castes or scheduled tribes can also contest a seat not reserved for them.

questions.subject: Polity
statementLines: Consider the following statements:
1. The Election Commission of India is a five-member body.
2. Union Ministry of Home Affairs decides the election schedule for the conduct of both general elections and bye-elections.
3. Election Commission resolves the disputes relating to splits/mergers of recognized political parties.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements: 1. The Election Commission of India is a five-member body. 2. Union Ministry of Home Affairs decides the election schedule for the conduct of both general elections and bye-elections. 3. Election Commission resolves the disputes relating to splits/mergers of recognized political parties. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer.

**Statement 1 is incorrect.**Election Commission**consists of a**Chief Election Commissioner**and such number of Election Commissioners as the President may deem it necessary subject to the law made by Parliament in this regard.** Currently, it consists of Chief Election Commissioner and two Election Commissioners.
**Statement 2 is incorrect.**Election Commission**(not Ministry of Home Affairs) decides election schedule for the conduct of both general elections and bye-elections.**

**Statement 3 is correct.**Election Commission**resolves the disputes relating to splits/mergers of recognized political parties.**

questions.subject: Polity
statementLines: The National Green Tribunal Act, 2010 was enacted in consonance with which of the following provisions of the Constitution of India?
1. Right to healthy environment, construed as a part of Right to life under Article 21
2. Provision of grants for raising the level of administration in the Scheduled Areas for the welfare of Scheduled Tribes under Article 275(1)
3. Powers and functions of Gram Sabha as mentioned under Article 243(A)
Select the correct answer using the codes given below:
questions.questionText: The National Green Tribunal Act, 2010 was enacted in consonance with which of the following provisions of the Constitution of India? 1. Right to healthy environment, construed as a part of Right to life under Article 21 2. Provision of grants for raising the level of administration in the Scheduled Areas for the welfare of Scheduled Tribes under Article 275(1) 3. Powers and functions of Gram Sabha as mentioned under Article 243(A) Select the correct answer using the codes given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. The **National Green Tribunal Act, 2010** was enacted to create the National Green Tribunal for the facilitation of the judicial administration.
The National Green Tribunal Act, **2010** specifically aims at the creation of a National Green Tribunal which would help in the reduction of the burden of cases from the higher judiciary and effectively and efficiently dispose of issues relating to the environment, including basic rights of the individual for free and safe environment, protection of the flora and fauna, etc.
This act was created in consonance with the need to effectively manage and divert the increasing chunk of pending cases from the other organs of the judiciary.
This tribunal was also created so that the basic right of the individuals to a safe environment can be safeguarded as part of **Article 21** of the Constitution.
As the aims of the National Green Tribunal suggest, there is a huge importance attached to **Article 21** of Constitution concerning environmental matters.
The Act was not enacted in consonance with the provisions of grants for raising the level of administration in the Scheduled Areas for the welfare of Scheduled Tribes under Article 275(1) and powers and functions of Gram Sabha as mentioned under Article 243(A)

questions.subject: Polity
statementLines: Consider the following statements:
1. Union Territories are not represented in the Rajya Sabha.
2. It is within the purview of the Chief Election Commissioner to adjudicate the election disputes.
3. According to the Constitution of India, the Parliament consists of the Lok Sabha and the Rajya Sabha only.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements: 1. Union Territories are not represented in the Rajya Sabha. 2. It is within the purview of the Chief Election Commissioner to adjudicate the election disputes. 3. According to the Constitution of India, the Parliament consists of the Lok Sabha and the Rajya Sabha only. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer.

**Statement 1 is incorrect.**Union Territories**does have representation in the**Rajya Sabha**.** Although, out of the nine union territories, only three (Delhi, Puducherry and Jammu & Kashmir) have representation in Rajya Sabha.
The populations of other six union territories are too small to have any representative in the Rajya Sabha. The maximum membership of Rajya Sabha as laid down in the Constitution is 250. The present strength, however, is 245 members of whom 233 are representatives of the States and Union territories and 12 are nominated by the President.
The allocation of seats to be filled by representatives of the States and Union territories as presently laid down in the Fourth Schedule.

**Statement 2 is incorrect. Chief Election Commissioner does not adjudicate the election disputes.** Special Election Tribunals are to be constituted at the regional level under **Article 329B** of the Constitution to ensure speedy disposal of election petitions and disputes within a stipulated period of six months.
However, currently no such tribunal has been established by the parliament. So, at present, election petitions are filed in the **High Court** of the particular state in which the election was conducted.
**Statement 3 is incorrect. According to the Constitution, Parliament consists of the President and the two Houses" Rajya Sabha and Lok Sabha.**

questions.subject: Polity
statementLines: With reference to the Delimitation Commission, consider the following statements:
1. The orders of the Delimitation Commission cannot be challenged in a Court of Law.
2. When the orders of the Delimitation Commission are laid before the Lok Sabha or State Legislative Assembly, they cannot effect any modifications in the orders.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: With reference to the Delimitation Commission, consider the following statements: 1. The orders of the Delimitation Commission cannot be challenged in a Court of Law. 2. When the orders of the Delimitation Commission are laid before the Lok Sabha or State Legislative Assembly, they cannot effect any modifications in the orders. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. The **Delimitation Commission** or Boundary Commission of India is a commission established by the Government of India under the provisions of the Delimitation Commission Act.
The main task of the commission is redrawing the boundaries of the various assembly and Lok Sabha constituencies based on a recent census. The representation from each State is not changed during this exercise. However, the number of SC and ST seats in a state are changed in accordance with the census.
Both Statements 1 and 2 are correct. The Commission is a powerful and independent body whose orders cannot be challenged in a court of law. The orders are laid before the Lok Sabha and the respective State Legislative Assemblies. However, modifications are not permitted.

questions.subject: Polity
statementLines: Consider the following statements:
1. Central Administrative Tribunal (CAT) was set up during the Prime Ministership of Lal Bahadur Shastri.
2. The Members of CAT are drawn from both judicial and administrative streams.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements: 1. Central Administrative Tribunal (CAT) was set up during the Prime Ministership of Lal Bahadur Shastri. 2. The Members of CAT are drawn from both judicial and administrative streams. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer.

**Statement 1 is incorrect. The**Central Administrative Tribunal (CAT)**has been established for adjudication of disputes with respect to recruitment and conditions of service of persons appointed to public services and posts** in connection with the affairs of the Union or other local authorities within the territory of India or under the control of Government of India.
CAT was set up in **1985** during Rajiv Gandhis tenure. This was done in pursuance of the amendment of Constitution of India by **Article 323A**.
**Statement 2 is correct. The Chairman of CAT who has been a sitting or retired Judge of a High Court heads the Central Administrative Tribunal.** At present, there are 66 Honble Members in various Benches of the Tribunal out of which 33 are Judicial Members, including the Honble Chairman and 33 are Administrative Members.
Subject to other provisions of the Act, a Bench consists of one Judicial Member and one Administrative Member.

questions.subject: Polity
statementLines: Consider the following statements:
1. The Commonwealth has no charter, treaty or constitution.
2. All the territories/ countries once under the British Empire (jurisdiction/rule/ mandate) automatically joined the Commonwealth as its members.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements: 1. The Commonwealth has no charter, treaty or constitution. 2. All the territories/ countries once under the British Empire (jurisdiction/rule/ mandate) automatically joined the Commonwealth as its members. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer.

**Statement 1 is correct: The**Commonwealth**is a voluntary association of member States and has no charter, treaty or constitution.**

**Statement 2 is incorrect: For becoming a member of the Commonwealth, a country needs to apply for its membership.** Further, not all the former British colonies are the member of Commonwealth like the USA.

questions.subject: Polity
statementLines: Which Article of the Constitution provides that every state shall endeavour to provide an adequate facility for instruction in mother-tongue at primary stage of education?
questions.questionText: Which Article of the Constitution provides that every state shall endeavour to provide an adequate facility for instruction in mother-tongue at primary stage of education?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **Article 350A** facilitates instruction in the mother tongue at primary stage of education.
The article 350A says that it shall be the endeavor of every State and of every local authority within the State to provide adequate facilities for instruction in the mother tongue at the primary stage of education to children belonging to linguistic minority groups.
It also says that the President may issue such directions to any State as he considers necessary or proper for securing the provision of such facilities.
**

Important Tips Some important articles of the Indian Constitution are following:
- Article 343-Official Languages of the Union.
- Article 345-Official Languages or languages of states.
- Article 348-Languages to be used in the Supreme court and in the High Courts.
- Article 351-Directive for the Development of the Hindi language

questions.subject: Polity
statementLines: Given below are two statements, one labelled as Assertion (A) and the other labelled as Reason (R):
Assertion (A): The reservation of thirty-three percent of seats for women in Parliament and State Legislature does not require a Constitutional amendment.
Reason (R): Political parties contesting elections can allocate thirty-three percent of seats they contest to women candidates without any Constitutional amendment.
In the context of the above two statements
which one of the following is correct?
questions.questionText: Given below are two statements, one labelled as Assertion (A) and the other labelled as Reason (R): Assertion (A): The reservation of thirty-three percent of seats for women in Parliament and State Legislature does not require a Constitutional amendment. Reason (R): Political parties contesting elections can allocate thirty-three percent of seats they contest to women candidates without any Constitutional amendment. In the context of the above two statements which one of the following is correct?

options.a: Both (A) and (R) are true, and (R) is the correct explanation of (A).
options.b: Both (A) and (R) are true, but (R) is not a correct explanation of (A).

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. Assertion (A) is false-The **reservation of 33% seats for women** in Parliament and State Legislature requires a constitutional amendment.
This is because the Constitution of India does not explicitly provide for reservation of seats for women. The **Constitution (108th Amendment) Bill, 2008** proposes to reserve one-third of all seats in the Lok Sabha and state legislative assemblies for women.
The allocation of reserved seats will be determined by an authority prescribed by Parliament. Reason (R) is true-Political parties can allocate 33% of seats they contest to women candidates without any constitutional amendment.
However, this does not mean that women will be guaranteed 33% of seats in Parliament and State Legislature. This is because the number of seats that women actually win will depend on the number of women candidates that are fielded by political parties and the number of votes that they win.

questions.subject: Polity
statementLines: Which one of the following is not a principle of Panchsheel?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is not a principle of Panchsheel?

options.c: Mutual respect for each other's territorial integrity and sovereignty
options.d: Mutual non-interference in each other's internal affairs

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. The **Panchsheel Agreement** is a pact of **"Five Principles of Peaceful Coexistence"**. The first formal declaration of these concepts was an Agreement on Trade and Commerce between India and Chinas Tibet region, signed on 29th April **1954**. It includes principles of mutual respect for territorial integrity, non-aggression, non-interference in internal affairs, equality & mutual benefit, and peaceful coexistence.
Non-alignment, a distinct policy, involves maintaining neutrality without aligning with major power blocs during the Cold War era and is not part of Panchsheel.
**

Important Tips-The 5 principles were emphasized by Prime Minister Jawaharlal Nehru and Premier Zhou Enlai in a broadcast speech made at the time of Asian Prime Ministers Conference in Colombo, Sri Lanka after signing the Sino-Indian Agreement in Beijing, **1954**.
- The five principles were subsequently modified in a form of a statement of ten principles issued in April **1955** at the historic Asian-African Conference in Bandung, Indonesia.
- The conference itself would lead to the foundation of the Non-Aligned Movement which gave shape to the idea that the post-colonial nations had something to offer to the bipolar world of the Cold War.

questions.subject: Polity
statementLines: Given below are two statements, one labelled as Assertion (A) and the other labelled as Reason (R):

Assertion (A): In India, the political parties which formed the governments represented the majority of seats secured in the elections to the House of the People at the Centre and the Legislative Assemblies in the States but not the majority of votes.
Reason (R): The result of elections based on the majority-vote-system is decided on the basis of the relative majority of votes secured.
In the context of the above two statements
which one of the following is correct?
questions.questionText: Given below are two statements, one labelled as Assertion (A) and the other labelled as Reason (R): Assertion (A): In India, the political parties which formed the governments represented the majority of seats secured in the elections to the House of the People at the Centre and the Legislative Assemblies in the States but not the majority of votes. Reason (R): The result of elections based on the majority-vote-system is decided on the basis of the relative majority of votes secured. In the context of the above two statements which one of the following is correct?

options.a: Both (A) and (R) are true, and (R) is the correct explanation of (A).
options.b: Both (A) and (R) are true, but (R) is not a correct explanation of (A).

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. Assertion (A) is true-In India, the political parties that form the government do not always have the majority of votes.
For example, in the **2014** Lok Sabha elections, the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) won 282 seats, which was only 31.1% of the total votes cast.
However, the BJP was able to form the government because it had the most seats. Reason (R) is true and (R) is the correct explanation of (A)
- The **majority-vote-system** is a system where the party with the most votes wins. However, this does not mean that the party with the most votes will also have the majority of seats.
This is because the number of seats a party wins is based on the number of votes it wins in each constituency. So, it is possible for a party to win the most votes overall, but not have the majority of seats.

questions.subject: Polity
statementLines: According to the United Nations Charter which of the following statements is not true regarding exercise of veto power in the Security Council?
questions.questionText: According to the United Nations Charter which of the following statements is not true regarding exercise of veto power in the Security Council?

options.a: For the Security Council decision on every procedural matter there should be 9 affirmative votes of its members including the 5 votes of five permanent members.
options.b: Every permanent member of the Security Council may by use of Veto Power, withhold acceptance on any decision.
options.c: The word 'Veto' has been used under Article 27 of the UN Charter so that any permanent member may stop a resolution going to be passed with a majority.
options.d: Any member of the Security Council may use Veto Power to stop a resolution going to be passed with a majority.

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. The **veto power** is only available to the five permanent members of the **UN Security Council**.
Any other member of the Security Council cannot use veto power.

**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. The **national calendar** based on the **Saka Era**, with Chaitra as its first month and a normal year of 365 days was adopted from 22 March **1957** along with the Gregorian calendar for the official purposes:
**

Important Tips-The **Vikrama era** (57 BC) is said in the **Jain** book Kalakacaryakatha to have been founded after a victory of King Vikramaditya over the Saka.
- The **Saka era** was started by Kanishka I from 78 CE.
- The **Hijri year** started from 622 AD.

questions.subject: Polity
statementLines: Which one of the following is correct about the languages mentioned in the Constitution of India?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is correct about the languages mentioned in the Constitution of India?

options.a: State can compel the linguistic minorities to choose their mother tongue as a medium of instruction in primary schools.
options.b: 'Angika' is now included in the 8th Schedule of the Constitution.
options.c: Hindi is National language of India.
options.d: Bodo is a recognized language in the 8th Schedule of the Constitution.

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. The **Eighth Schedule** of the Indian Constitution includes 22 languages. Originally, it had 14 languages but presently there are 22 languages. The 22 languages are (1) Assamese, (2) Bengali, (3) Gujarati, (4) Hindi, (5) Kannada, (6) Kashmiri, (7) Urdu (8) Malayalam, (9) Telugu, (10) Marathi, (11) Tamil,(12) Oriya, (13) Punjabi, (14) Sanskrit, (15) Sindhi, (16) Nepali,(17) Manipuri, (18) Konkani,(19) Bodo, (20) Santhali, (21) Maithili and (22) Dogri.
**

Important Tips Eighth Schedule:
- The Eighth Schedule to the Constitution of India lists the official languages of India.
The Eighth Schedule recognises a total of 22 languages.
- However, it can be noted that there are no fixed criteria for any language to be considered for inclusion in the Eighth Schedule.
- Part XVII of the Indian constitution deals with the official languages in Articles 343 to 351.
- The Constitutional provisions relating to the Eighth Schedule occur in Articles 344(1) and 351 of the Constitution.

statementLines: Study the following statements and
select the correct answer from the code given below:

Reason (R): Growth of strong regional parties is an indicator of regional aspirations.

questions.questionText: Study the following statements and select the correct answer from the code given below: Assertion (A): India has a written Constitution. Reason (R): Growth of strong regional parties is an indicator of regional aspirations. Code:

options.b: Both (A) and (R) are true, but (R) is not the correct explanation of (A).

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. Assertion (A) is true-India does have a **written constitution**. The Constitution of India was adopted on 26 January **1950**, and it is the supreme law of the land. Reason (R) is true, but (R) is not the correct explanation of (A)
- The growth of strong **regional parties** is an indicator of regional aspirations.
This means that people in different regions of India have different needs and interests, and they want to be represented by political parties that will fight for their interests.
However, The fact that India has a written constitution does not necessarily mean that there will be strong regional parties. There are many other factors that can contribute to the growth of regional parties, such as economic inequality, linguistic diversity, and historical factors.

questions.subject: Polity
statementLines: The Indian Foreign Policy of Non-Alignment was initiated by-
questions.questionText: The Indian Foreign Policy of Non-Alignment was initiated by-
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. The policy of **non-alignment** was first articulated by **Jawaharlal Nehru**, Indias first Prime Minister, in the early years of Indias independence.
Nehru believed that India should not align itself with either the Western bloc or the Eastern bloc, but should instead pursue an independent foreign policy based on the principles of peace, cooperation, and non-violence.

questions.subject: Polity
statementLines: Which of the following is a reason of emergence of regionalism in India?
questions.questionText: Which of the following is a reason of emergence of regionalism in India?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. Causes of **regionalism in India**:
- Uneven development: Disparities in economic, social, and infrastructure development between regions lead to resentment and frustration.
- Fear of losing cultural identity: Globalization and homogenization create a fear of losing diverse cultural identity, sparking regionalism to protect it.
- Political prominence: Rise of regional parties in Indian politics empowers leaders to advocate for their constituents concerns and demand more resources for their regions.

statementLines: Consider the following statements:

questions.questionText: Consider the following statements: 1. The design of the National Flag was adopted by the Constituent Assembly of India on 22nd July, 1947 2. The wheel in National Flag has 21 spokes 3. The ratio of the width of the National Flag to its length is 3: 4 Which of the above statements is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. The **National Flag of India** Tiranga i.e., having three colors in a rectangular shape. Saffron in the top, White with Navy blue wheel having 24 spokes in Center and Green in the bottom. It was adopted by the Constituent Assembly on 22 July **1947** and became official National Flag when India became Republic.
The ratio of the width of the National Flag to its length is 2: 3. **

Important Tips-The top saffron colour indicates the strength and courage of the country.
- The white middle band indicates peace and truth with Dharma Chakra.
- The green shows the fertility, growth and auspiciousness of the land.

questions.subject: Polity
statementLines: Which is the correct chronological order of the following?
1. Shimla Agreement
2. Indo-Soviet Treaty
3. Farakka Accord
4. Tashkent Agreement
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.questionText: Which is the correct chronological order of the following? 1. Shimla Agreement 2. Indo-Soviet Treaty 3. Farakka Accord 4. Tashkent Agreement Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer.
- **Tashkent Agreement (**1966**)**: India-Pakistan accord after the **1965** war, called for a ceasefire and troop withdrawal from the border.
- **Indo-Soviet Treaty (**1971**)**: Friendship and cooperation treaty with the Soviet Union.
- **Shimla Agreement (**1972**)**: India-Pakistan pact after the Bangladesh Liberation War, establishing the LOC and promoting peaceful dispute resolution.
- **Farakka Accord (**1977**)**: India-Bangladesh agreement aimed to resolve the water-sharing dispute related to the Ganges River.

questions.subject: Polity
statementLines: Meaning of Extra-Constitutional Right-
questions.questionText: Meaning of Extra-Constitutional Right-

options.a: That power which violates the limits of Constitution.
options.b: A person who has got exceptional powers under the Constitution.
options.c: That power which is not in accordance with the provisions of the Constitution.
options.d: That official who has all the powers of the State during the proclamation of emergency.

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **Extra-constitutional right** refers to a power that is not explicitly granted by the constitution.
Such powers may be derived from custom, tradition, or other sources of law.

questions.subject: Polity
statementLines: Under Article 344 of Indian Constitution, the first 'Official Language Commission' was established-
questions.questionText: Under Article 344 of Indian Constitution, the first 'Official Language Commission' was established-

options.a: In 1950 under the Chairmanship of K.M. Munshi
options.b: In 1955 Under the Chairmanship of B.G. Kher
options.c: In 1960 under the Chairmanship of M.C. Chagala
options.d: In 1965 under the Chairmanship of Humayun Kabir

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. **Part XVII** of the Constitution deals with the official language in Articles 343 to 351. The first Official Language Commission was formed in **1955** under the chairmanship of B.G.
Kher. The commission submitted its report to the President in **1956**. The report was examined by a committee of Parliament constituted in **1957** under the chairmanship of Gobind Ballabh Pant.
**

Important Tips
- **Article 344(1)** provides for the constitution of a Commission by the President on expiration of five years from the commencement of the Constitution and thereafter at the expiration of ten years from such commencement.
This commission shall consist of a Chairman and such other members representing the different languages specified in the Eighth Schedule to make recommendations to the President for the progressive use of Hindi for official purposes of the Union.
- The Official Language Commission (as envisaged by the Constitution) was not appointed in **1960**.

questions.subject: Polity
statementLines: Inner-Party Democracy stands for-
questions.questionText: Inner-Party Democracy stands for-

options.a: A Government formed by several parties with a common programme
options.b: Party closest to the party or parties forming the Government
options.c: Periodical elections within the party to elect the office bearers of the party
options.d: Secrets of Government are shared by parties in power

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **Inner-party democracy** is a system of governance within a political party that allows for the participation of its members in the decision-making process. This includes the right to elect party leaders, to participate in party debates, and to hold the party leadership accountable.
Periodical elections within the party to elect the office bearers of the party is one of the key features of inner-party democracy. This ensures that the party leadership is accountable to the party members and that the party is responsive to the needs of the people.

questions.subject: Polity
statementLines: The Press Council of India is a-
questions.questionText: The Press Council of India is a-
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. The **Press Council of India (PCI)** is a statutory body established under the Press Council Act, **1978**, serving as a self-regulatory body for Indias print media.
It investigates complaints, issues directions for corrections and apologies, and recommends actions against journalists. Though not a judicial body, it holds quasi-judicial powers, conducting hearings and issuing legally enforceable orders.

questions.subject: Polity
statementLines: When the Hindi version of National Anthem Jana-Gana-Mana was adopted by the Constitution Assembly of India?
questions.questionText: When the Hindi version of National Anthem Jana-Gana-Mana was adopted by the Constitution Assembly of India?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. The National Anthem of India **Jana-gana-mana**, composed originally in Bengali by **Rabindranath Tagore**, was adopted in its Hindi version by the Constituent Assembly as the National Anthem oflndia on 24 January **1950**.
**

Important Tips National Anthem:
- It was first sung on 27 December **1911** at the Kolkata Session of the Indian National Congress.
- Rabindranath Tagore had published it in Tatvabodhini in **1912** with the title Bharat Bhagya Vidhata and translated it into English in **1919** with the title Morning song of India.
- The credit of composing the present tune (music) of our national anthem goes to Captain Ram Singh Thakur (an I.N.A. sepoy).

questions.subject: Polity
statementLines: Who was the Prime Minister of India when the basic principle of India's Nuclear Doctrine of 'No First Use' was adopted?
questions.questionText: Who was the Prime Minister of India when the basic principle of India's Nuclear Doctrine of 'No First Use' was adopted?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. The **'No First Use' policy** means that India will not be the first to use nuclear weapons in any conflict or war.
This policy was officially declared by India in **1999** under the leadership of Prime Minister **Atal Bihari Vajpayee**. It reflects Indias commitment to using nuclear weapons solely as a deterrent against aggression and not for initiating hostilities.

questions.subject: Polity
statementLines: Who recognizes the political parties in India?
questions.questionText: Who recognizes the political parties in India?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. The **Election Commission of India (ECI)** is an autonomous constitutional body responsible for conducting elections to the Lok Sabha, Rajya Sabha, state legislative assemblies, and local bodies in India.
The ECI recognizes political parties in India based on certain objective criteria. **

Important Tips As per the ECTs Political Parties and Election Symbols, **2019** handbook, a political party can be recognized as a **national party** if it meets any of the following conditions:
- It is recognized in four or more states.
- Its candidates have secured at least 6% of the total valid votes in a minimum of 4 states during the latest Lok Sabha or Assembly elections, and the party has at least 4 Members of Parliament (MPs) from the last Lok Sabha polls.
- It has won at least 2% of the total seats in the Lok Sabha from a minimum of 3 states.
- Currently, Seven Political Parties in India are recognized as National Party by the Election Commission oflndia

questions.subject: Polity
statementLines: What does the wheel on the 'Indian National Flag' symbolize?
questions.questionText: What does the wheel on the 'Indian National Flag' symbolize?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. The wheel at the centre of our National Flag is known as **Ashoka Chakra**. Our National Flag was composed by **Pingali Venkayya**. The blue colour denotes the colour of the sky and ocean. The wheel in the national flag represent Motion.
This wheel is also known as wheel of the law of dharma/ Justice. Truth or satya, dharma or virtue ought to be the controlling principle of those who work under this flag.

questions.subject: Polity
statementLines: With reference to the UN peacekeeping operations, consider the following statements:
1. UN peacekeepers can use force only for their self-defense.
2. Peacekeeping operations can only be withdrawn by a resolution by the UN Security Council.
3. USA is the largest budget contributor to the UN peacekeeping operations.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.questionText: With reference to the UN peacekeeping operations, consider the following statements: 1. UN peacekeepers can use force only for their self-defense. 2. Peacekeeping operations can only be withdrawn by a resolution by the UN Security Council. 3. USA is the largest budget contributor to the UN peacekeeping operations. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer.

**Statement 1 is incorrect:**UN peacekeepers**are not only authorized to use force in self-defense or in defense of the mandate given to them by the UNSC, but in certain volatile situations, the UNSC has given UN peacekeeping operations robust mandates authorizing them to use all necessary means to deter forceful attempts to disrupt the political process, protect civilians under imminent threat of physical attack, and/or assist the national authorities in maintaining law and order.**

**Statement 2 is correct: The**UN Security Council**is responsible for authorizing and overseeing peacekeeping missions, and its decisions determine the continuation or termination of such operations.**

**Statement 3 is correct: The**USA**is the largest budget contributor to UN peacekeeping operations, providing the highest percentage (28% in**2021**) of assessed contributions among all member states.**

questions.subject: Polity
statementLines: Who was among the following to receive the first Bharat Ratna?
questions.questionText: Who was among the following to receive the first Bharat Ratna?

options.e: None of the above/ More than one of the above

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. The **Bharat Ratna** is the highest civilian award of the Republic of India. Instituted on 2 January **1954**, the award is conferred in recognition of exceptional service/performance of the highest order, without distinction of race, occupation, position, or sex.
The First Recipient of the Bharat Ratna was **Dr. Sarvepalli Radhakrishnan**, who received this esteemed award in **1954** for his distinguished services as a philosopher, statesman, and academic.
**

Important Tips Additional facts on Bharat Ratna Awards-On the Prime Ministers suggestion to the President of India, a maximum of three Bharat Ratna awards can be presented each year.
- The recipient of the award receives a Sanad (certificate) signed by the President as well as a medallion. The prize does not come with a monetary grant.
- The awardees are not allowed to use Bharat Ratna as a prefix or suffix to their names, as stated in Article 18(1) of the Indian Constitution.
- There is no formal requirement that the Bharat Ratna be granted solely to Indian nationals. This prestigious prize was given to a naturalized Indian citizen, Agnes Gonxha Bojaxhiu, popularly known as Mother Teresa (**1980**), as well as non-Indians, Khan Abdul Ghaffar Khan (**1987**) and Nelson Mandela (**1990**).
- Posthumous honours were not permitted under the **1954** legislation, but this was changed in the January **1955** act, and Lal Bahadur Shastri became the first winner to be honoured posthumously in **1966**.

questions.subject: Polity
statementLines: Consider the following statements about Electoral Bond Scheme 2018:
questions.questionText: Consider the following statements about Electoral Bond Scheme 2018:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. Statement A is correct

The **Electoral Bond Scheme** was introduced by the Government of India in **2018**. The aim of the scheme is to bring about transparency in the funding process of political parties.
Statement B is correct

Only political parties recognized by the Election Commission which secured not less than two percent of the votes polled in the last general election to the House of People or the Legislative Assembly of the State shall be eligible to receive the Electoral Bonds.
Statement C is correct

The scheme allows individuals and companies to donate money to political parties through Electoral Bonds. Electoral Bonds are bonds that are issued by the government and can be purchased by individuals and companies. The bonds are valid for fifteen calendar days from the date of issue.
Statement D is correct

The Electoral Bond deposited by an eligible political party in its account shall be credited on the same day.

questions.subject: Polity
statementLines: Who has authored 'Gandhian Constitution for Free India'?
questions.questionText: Who has authored 'Gandhian Constitution for Free India'?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. The **Gandhian Constitution for Free India** was a draft constitution that was prepared by **Shriman Narayan Agarwal** in the early 1940s.
The constitution was based on the principles of Gandhian economics and social justice. It was never adopted by the Indian government, but it remains an important document in the history of Indian political thought.

questions.subject: Polity
statementLines: The powers of District Collector as a District Magistrate are:
questions.questionText: The powers of District Collector as a District Magistrate are:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. The **District Magistrate** is a judicial officer, while the **District Collector** is an executive officer.
In the role of a magistrate DC has the powers to maintain law and order, control the police, and check the passports of foreigners. He/she also has the power to control land revenue, but this is under the role of Collector, not as the Magistrate. **

Important Tips Other functions of DC as a Magistrate:
- As District Magistrate, the powers and responsibilities include issuance of adoption orders under the Juvenile Justice Act, granting arms and ammunition licenses, and cinema licenses, all subject to appeal to divisional commissioners.
- They conduct criminal courts as an executive magistrate, and supervise subordinate executive magistracy.
- Additionally, they handle cases under the preventive section of the Criminal Procedure Code, oversee jails, and certify capital sentence executions.
- Their duties involve inspecting police stations, prisons, and juvenile homes, authorizing parole orders, preparing panels for appointing public prosecutors, and managing crises during riots, and issues related to child labor and bonded labor.

questions.subject: Polity
statementLines: What is true about gallantry award 'Param Vir Chakra'?
questions.questionText: What is true about gallantry award 'Param Vir Chakra'?

options.a: This award can be given to an individual only once in his/her lifetime
options.b: On its medal, four replicas of 'Indra Vajra' are embossed
options.c: Till 31st December 2021, 25 people have been awarded with Param Vir Chakra
options.d: This award was instituted on 26th January 1952

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. The **Param Vir Chakra**, highest gallantry award in India, features a circular bronze disc 35 millimetres in diameter.
The obverse side displays the National Emblem of India at the center, surrounded by four copies of the vajra, the weapon of Indra, the ancient Vedic king of the gods.
This motif symbolizes the sacrifice of Rishi Dadhichi, who gave his bones to make the vajra to kill the demon Vritra. **

Important Tips Gallantry Awards in India:
- Param Vir Chakra is the highest wartime award for exceptional valor, followed by Maha Vir Chakra and Vir Chakra.
- Ashoka Chakra tops peacetime awards for bravery or sacrifice, followed by Kirti Chakra and Shaurya Chakra.
- Additional awards like Sena Medal, Nao Sena Medal, and Vayu Sena Medal are bestowed for acts of courage or devotion to duty in their respective services.
- The first recipient was Major Somnath Sharma for his actions in the 1947-48**India-Pakistan War and the last recipient was Captain Vikram Batra for actions in the Kargil War in**1999**. - Till now it has been granted to 21 armed forces personnel. - It was designed by**Savitri Khanolkar**, a Swiss national whose real name was Eve Yvonne Maday de Maros.

questions.subject: Polity
statementLines: What is not true in reference to Chief of Defence Staff?
questions.questionText: What is not true in reference to Chief of Defence Staff?

options.a: Maximum age limit for this post is 65 years
options.b: He is permanent Chairman of Chief of Staff Committee
options.c: He is Chairman of Defence Acquisition Council
options.d: He is member of Defence Planning Committee

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. The **DAC** is the highest decision-making body in the Defence Ministry for deciding on new policies and capital acquisitions for the three services (Army, Navy and Air Force) and the Indian Coast Guard.
The defence minister is the chairman of DAC. Its members include the **Chief of Defence Staff (CDS)** and Chiefs of the Army, Navy, and Air Force.
**

Important Tips-The CDS holds the highest military rank in India and provides single-point military advice to the government.
- Appointed by the President on the advice of the Prime Minister, the CDS is a four-star general and serves as the permanent Chairman of the Chiefs of Staff Committee (COSC).
- The CDS is a member of the Defence Planning Committee (DPC) responsible for formulating longterm defence plans.
- As the head of the Department of Military Affairs (DMA) in the Ministry of Defence, the CDS acts as the single-point adviser to the Defence Minister.
- In the Department of Defence (DoD), the CDS holds the rank of Secretary and exercises powers limited to the revenue budget.
Additionally, the CDS performs an advisory role in the Nuclear Command Authority (NCA).

questions.subject: Polity
statementLines: Which of the following may be considered a Pressure Group?
questions.questionText: Which of the following may be considered a Pressure Group?

**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **Scientific socialism** is attributed to **Karl Marx**. He, along with Friedrich Engels, developed the concept of scientific socialism, which forms the theoretical foundation of Marxism.
It emphasizes the scientific analysis of society, historical materialism, and the inevitable transition from capitalism to communism. **

Important Tips-Scientific socialism employs the scientific method to analyze historical trends, predict social and economic outcomes, and understand material phenomena.
It differs from utopian socialism and classical liberalism, focusing on materialist explanations rather than metaphysical or moral notions.
- Some of the key tenets of scientific socialism include:
- The materialist conception of history: This is the belief that the material conditions of life, such as the way people produce and distribute goods and services, determine the social, political, and intellectual development of society.
- The class struggle: This is the belief that society is divided into two main classes, the bourgeoisie and the proletariat.
The bourgeoisie are the owners of the means of production, while the proletariat are the workers who sell their labor to the bourgeoisie.
- The dictatorship of the proletariat: This is the belief that the working class will eventually overthrow the capitalist system and establish a socialist society.
The socialist society will be a classless society in which the means of production are owned and controlled by the working class.

statementLines: Which one of the following statements about 'personal liberty' is not correct?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements about 'personal liberty' is not correct?

options.a: State does not have the authority to deprive any person within the Territory of India of his/her personal liberty without any rational basis.
options.b: Basis of depriving a person of his/her personal liberty must be in accordance with procedures established by law.
options.c: Personal liberty can be secured by the judicial writ of Habeas Corpus.
options.d: The majority view of the Supreme Court in A.K. Gopalan v/s. State of Madras case invented 'due process of law'.

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. **"Due process of law"** was already a part of the Indian legal system, inherited from the British common law tradition.
The majority view in the **A.K. Gopalan case** held that the right to due process of law was not a fundamental right under the Constitution of India, allowing the state to deprive a person of their personal liberty without following established legal procedures, as long as the deprivation was not arbitrary or mala fide.
However, the minority view in the same case argued that due process of law was indeed a fundamental right, necessitating adherence to established legal procedures even if the deprivation was not arbitrary or mala fide.
Subsequently, the Supreme Court overruled its decision in the A.K. Gopalan case in the **Maneka Gandhi case**, holding that the right to due process of law is indeed a fundamental right.
Consequently, the state can only deprive a person of their personal liberty if it adheres to the procedures established by law.

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. One of the key features of **polyarchy** is that the right to run for office is not restricted.
In other words, anyone who meets the legal requirements should be able to run for office, regardless of their race, gender, religion, or social class.
**

Important Tips In his **1989** book Democracy and Its Critics, **Robert Dahl** gives the following characteristics of a polyarchy:
- Control over governmental decisions about policy is constitutionally vested in elected officials.
- Elected officials are chosen and peacefully removed in relatively frequent, fair and free elections in which coercion is quite limited.
- Practically all adults have the right to vote in these elections.
- Most adults also have the right to run for the public offices for which candidates run in these elections.
- Citizens have an effectively enforced right to freedom of expression, particularly political expression, including criticism of the officials, the conduct of the government, the prevailing political, economic, and social system, and the dominant ideology.
- They also have access to alternative sources of information that are not monopolized by the government or any other single group.

**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. While there are numerous political parties in India that are based on regional interests and have a strong presence in specific states or regions, the Election Commission does not specifically classify them as Regional Parties.
The classification system mainly focuses on recognizing parties at the national and state levels based on their electoral performance and adherence to certain criteria.

questions.subject: Polity
statementLines: The 'Gujral Doctrine' relates to
which one of the following Issues?
questions.questionText: The 'Gujral Doctrine' relates to which one of the following Issues?

options.a: Build trust between India and its neighbours
options.b: Initiate dialogue with all insurgent groups in India
options.c: Undertake development activities in Naxal-dominated areas

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer.
- This doctrine is considered as epitome of Indias foreign policy-It was propounded by IK GUJRAL, the 12th PM of India.
- The doctrine advocates that India, being the biggest country in South Asia, should extend unilateral concessions to the smaller neighbours.
- In other words, the doctrine is formulated on Indias accommodating approach towards its smaller neighbours on the basis of the principle of non-reciprocity.
- It recognises the supreme importance of friendly and cordial relations with Indias neighbours.

questions.subject: Polity
statementLines: The Prime Minister's National Relief Fund is operated by
which one of the following bodies?
questions.questionText: The Prime Minister's National Relief Fund is operated by which one of the following bodies?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer.
- Established in: **1948**
- Used for: immediate relief to families of those killed in natural calamities like floods, cyclones and earthquakes, etc. and to the victims of the major accidents and riots-Headed by: Prime Minister-Administration: Administered on an Honorary basis by Joint Secretary to the Prime Minister as Secretary of the fund.
- PMNRF operates from the Prime Ministers Office (PMO) (
option a is correct) Features: (a) Consists entirely of public contributions and does not get any budgetary support. (b) Recognized as a Trust under the Income Tax Act (c) Exempt under the Income Tax Act, **1961** under Section 10 and 139 for return purposes.

questions.subject: Polity
statementLines: Which one of the following is not an International Human Rights Treaty?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is not an International Human Rights Treaty?

options.a: International Covenant on Civil and Political Rights
options.b: Convention of the Elimination of All Forms of Discrimination against Women
options.c: Convention on the Rights of Persons with Disabilities
options.d: Declaration on the Right to Development

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. International Human Rights Treaty consists of 9 core treaties that are responsible for the international system for the promotion and protection of human rights.
**

Important Tips There are seven core international human rights treaties: 1. International Convention on the Elimination of All Forms of Racial Discrimination (ICERD)
- **1965**
2. International Covenant on Civil and Political Rights (ICCPR)
- **1966**
3. International Covenant on Economic, Social and Cultural Rights (ICESCR)
- **1966**
4. Convention on the Elimination of All Forms of Discrimination against Women (CEDAW)
- **1979**
5. Convention against Torture and Other Cruel, Inhuman or Degrading Treatment or Punishment (CAT)
- **1984**
6. Convention on the Rights of the Child (CRC)
- **1989**
7. International Convention on Protection of the Rights of All Migrant Workers and Members of Their Families (ICMRW)
- **2003**

questions.subject: Polity
statementLines: According to the Election Commission of India, in order to be recognised as a 'National Party', a political party must be as a recognised as a state party in how many states?
questions.questionText: According to the Election Commission of India, in order to be recognised as a 'National Party', a political party must be as a recognised as a state party in how many states?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. As per the ECTs Political Parties and Election Symbols, **2019** handbook, a political party would be considered a national party if:
i. it is recognised in four or more states; or ii. if its candidates polled at least 6% of total valid votes in any four or more states in the last Lok Sabha or iii. Assembly elections and has at least four MPs in the last Lok Sabha polls; or iv. If it has won at least 2% of the total seats in the Lok Sabha from not less than three states.

questions.subject: Polity
statementLines: Which one of the following intellectual property rights is protected without making any registration?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following intellectual property rights is protected without making any registration?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer.
- Copyrights: A copyright exists from the moment the work get created, so registration is voluntary hence it is protected without making any registration-Patent: A patent is used to prevent an invention from being created sold or used by another party without permission-Industrial design: An industrial design right is an intellectual property right that protects the visual design of objects that are purely utilitarian.
- Trademark: A trademark is a type of intellectual property consisting of a recognizable sign, design, or expression that identifies products or services from a particular source and distinguishes them from others.
